title: Last Stone Weight
problem_statement: Smash the two heaviest stones until at most one remains, and return its weight.

examples.explanation: Smash 8 and 7 => 1, then 4 and 2 => 2, then 2 and 1 => 1.

intuition: Always smash the two heaviest stones, so use a max-heap to efficiently retrieve them.

approaches.name: Max-heap simulation
approaches.description: Push all stones into a max-heap, repeatedly pop the two largest, and push the difference if nonzero.
approaches.why_not: Sorting each round is slower than using a heap to maintain the heaviest stones.

interview_qa.a: The two heaviest stones are needed each round, so a max-heap gives them efficiently.

interview_qa.q: What happens when the two stones are equal?
interview_qa.a: Both are destroyed and no stone is pushed back into the heap.
